summary: Excellent soundboard capture of the late‑1973 Denver show with crisp instrument mix and a smoking To Lay Me Down → Mind Left Body jam.
review: The performance is widely praised as one of the high points of the band’s 1973 run, highlighted by standout renditions of “To Lay Me Down,” “Dire Wolf,” “Stella,” and the seamless China > Rider transition that rolls into a flawless Mind Left Body jam. The recording itself is described as crystal‑clear with piano up front and very little hiss, though a few listeners note low vocal levels and occasional stereo‑spread quirks in certain copies.

ai_rating.rationale: The overwhelming majority of reviews are 5‑star, emphasizing both superb musical performance and high‑quality soundboard recording; only a single 3‑star comment mentions vocal balance, so the weighted average is strongly positive.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clean mix with all instruments well balanced; Keith’s piano sits forward, Phil’s bass “bombs away,” and minimal hiss. Vocals sit a bit low for some listeners, and one copy was reported to have an off‑center stereo spread, but overall the sound is considered superb for a 1973 analog feed.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers rave about the late‑'73 improvement, noting a particularly strong second set with flawless transitions (e.g., China > Rider into Stella) and an extended Mind Left Body jam that many call one of the grooviest moments ever captured.

band_member_comments.Jerry: A few reviewers note Jerry briefly lagging behind the band during the Mind Left Body jam, sparking discussion about his timing; overall his tone is praised as strong.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass is highlighted repeatedly as powerful and clear – “bombs away” with no hiss.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob’s rhythm work is credited for driving The Other One and for leading the Mind Left Body jam, described as inventive and “grooviest”.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s piano is specifically called out for being up in the mix and audible, a rarity on many recordings of the era.
